content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (one of the descriptors in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List them with `ls .claude/tools/` and run `<path> --help` to learn each tool's purpose and usage. Invoke them via the Bash tool. Project MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning. Useful when you need a second opinion or a paper-style proof sketch the Lean side can then formalize.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (see "Subagents" below). One wrapper handles every subagent.
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`. Each starts with YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`) followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude session reads.
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents yourself.** The plan and review prompts have an auto-generated **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation that lists every enabled descriptor with its description, write-domain hint, and any `[MANDATORY]` flags. When you decide to invoke a specific subagent, read `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` for its full prompt and directive shape.
31	
32	**Invoke any subagent with the generic wrapper** (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, and writes the subagent's report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. It shells out to `archon subagent <name>`, so executions stream through the Archon JSONL log like a phase agent. The dispatch semaphore bounds total concurrent subagent processes by `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` automatically so deeper-nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Always dispatch synchronously** (not via background `Bash` with `run_in_background: true`). The wrapper returns when the subagent has finished and the report is on disk; only then write your session summary. Background dispatch leaves the parent's session permanently summarized as "running in background", which is wrong as soon as the subagent completes.
45	
46	**Mandatory subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` MUST be dispatched at least once during the plan phase (similarly for `[review]`). The catalog tags them `[MANDATORY]`. A post-phase audit warns when a mandatory dispatch is missing.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All state files are in `.archon/`:
51	
52	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents | Review Agent | User |
53	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
54	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
55	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
56	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
57	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
58	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
59	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<name>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
60	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
61	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log moved to iter/iter-NNN/review.md) | read |
62	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | do not access | do not access | read (last K iters as context) | read |
63	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (last K iters as context) | do not access | do not access | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write (per-attempt detail) | do not access | do not access | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (write-capable subagents may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | per descriptor: write-capable subagents (e.g. refactor) may edit per their declared write-domain (protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); read-only subagents do not edit | do not edit | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
68	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not edit | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | write-capable when the descriptor's write_domain covers it | do not access | read |
70	
71	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent (when a `refactor` descriptor is installed) via the generic `archon-subagent.py --name refactor` wrapper with a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
72	
73	## Protected Declarations
74	
75	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
76	
77	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
78	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
79	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
80	
81	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
82	
83	The blueprint uses two active markers.
84	
85	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
86	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
87	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
88	
89	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
90	
91	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
92	
93	## User Interaction
94	
95	Users provide hints in two places:
96	
97	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
98	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
99	
100	## Agent Roles
101	
102	### Plan Agent
103	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
104	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
105	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
106	- Optionally invoke **subagents** (discoverable via `ls .archon/subagents/`) by running `.claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> ...` via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for the canonical ordering and full invocation pattern.
107	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
108	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
109	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
110	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
111	
112	### Prover Agent
113	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
114	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
115	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
116	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
117	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
118	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
119	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
120	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
121	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
122	
123	### Subagents
124	
125	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime — there is no fixed roster baked into this file. Each lives at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter + prompt body). To see what's available **this iteration**, run `ls .archon/subagents/` and read each descriptor's frontmatter for purpose, write-domain hint, and directive shape.
126	
127	Subagents are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. Phase agents (plan, review) invoke them on demand via the generic wrapper:
128	
129	```
130	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name <name> --slug <slug> --directive-file <path> --write-domain '<glob>'
131	```
132	
133	Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. The dispatching agent reads the report when the wrapper returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings.
134	
135	Some descriptors set `can_spawn: true`, meaning that subagent may itself dispatch children; the per-iter dispatch semaphore caps total concurrent processes by `loop.max_parallel` so deep trees stay bounded.
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
139	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
140	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
141	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
142	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init**: at iter start, `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` is created so the plan + review agents have a stable destination for their per-iter narrative (`plan.md`, `review.md`, optional `objectives.md`). Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded across iters because per-iter content lives in the sidecars.
150	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
151	
152	`sync_leanok` writes an inner-git commit (`archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so its output is auditable and revertable.

55	## Current Objectives

58	
59	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — close `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` trailing sorry at L1147
60	
61	**File status (iter-106 entry).** 6 sorries; FILE COMPILES. Foundation
62	chain (all on disk, all verified):
63

86	start of work.
87	
88	### Step 1 — close L1147 via Route 1 (primary) or Route 3 (fallback)
89	
90	**The iter-105 prover left a structured partial proof at L1119–L1147**
91	which establishes (after the iter-101/103 S1–S5 scalar-extraction pivots):
92	
93	```lean
94	have hRel' : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2 = n + 1 := by omega
95	have h_wrap := cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear hU s₀ n hn (Fin.cast hRel' i)
96	have h_wrap_pt := congrFun (h_wrap r' y') j'
97	simp only [Pi.smul_apply] at h_wrap_pt
98	-- h_wrap_pt now reads (after ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply
99	--   unfolds e₂ X j' = (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom X):
100	--   (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom ((wrapper at (Fin.cast hRel' i)).hom (e₁.symm (r' • y')))
101	--     = r' • (Pi.π Z₂ j').hom ((wrapper at (Fin.cast hRel' i)).hom (e₁.symm y'))
102	-- Goal at L1147 differs by having eqToHom_outer.hom ∘ ((-1)^↑i • F_at_i).hom
103	-- instead of wrapper.hom, where F_at_i is the literal Pi.lift at index i.
104	sorry
105	```
106	
107	The remaining gap is the morphism-level identification:
108	```
109	F_at_i ≫ eqToHom_outer = cechCofaceMap_summand_family' s₀ n hn i
110	```
111	(or its evaluation at coord `j'`), modulo pulling `(-1)^↑i •` out through
112	the k-linear maps `Pi.π Z₂ j'` and `eqToHom_outer` and commuting it past
113	`r' •` via `smul_comm` (R- and ℤ-actions on Z₂ j').
114	
115	#### Route 1 (PRIMARY) — add a top-level morphism-equality lemma
116	
117	Insert a new top-level lemma after `cechCofaceMap_summand_family'_R_linear`
118	(near L727, before `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux`):
119	
120	```lean
121	/-- Iter-106: the iter-104 named family at `Fin.cast hRel.symm i` composed with
122	the canonical `eqToHom` transport `∏ᶜ Z_int → ∏ᶜ Z₂` equals the iter-105
123	wrapper at `i`. Used in `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` to rewrite the anonymous-
124	closure form into a named wrapper before applying the wrapper R-linearity. -/
125	theorem cechCofaceMap_summand_family_comp_eqToHom_eq_summand_family'
126	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
127	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
128	    (s₀ : Finset Γ(C.left, U)) (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
129	    let hRel : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 2 = n + 1 := by
130	      rcases n with _ | k
131	      · omega
132	      · simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]
133	    (cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n (Fin.cast hRel.symm i)) ≫
134	      eqToHom (by congr 1; funext j; congr 1; funext a; rw [hRel])
135	      = cechCofaceMap_summand_family' s₀ n hn i := by
136	  -- Proof sketch (~10–20 LOC):
137	  -- 1. Reduce to per-coord equality via Pi.hom_ext / Limits.limit.hom_ext.
138	  -- 2. funext j_new (the codomain index `Fin (n + 1) → ↑s₀`).
139	  -- 3. On the LHS: rw [Category.assoc, eqToHom_naturality / Pi.π_eqToHom],
140	  --    then Pi.lift_π_apply on the named family.
141	  -- 4. On the RHS: unfold cechCofaceMap_summand_family',
142	  --    Pi.lift_π_apply on the wrapper.
143	  -- 5. Both sides reduce to (Pi.π Z₁ <some_index>) ≫ (toModuleKPresheaf C).map _.op
144	  --    where the two indices coincide via Fin.cast_cast roundtrip + funext.
145	  sorry
146	```
147	
148	After this lemma compiles + proves, replace L1147 sorry with:
149	
150	```lean
151	-- Pull (-1)^↑i • out through Pi.π Z₂ j' and eqToHom_outer (both k-linear)
152	-- and commute past r' • via smul_comm. Then rewrite F_at_i ≫ eqToHom_outer
153	-- to the wrapper via the new lemma, and apply h_wrap_pt.
154	have h_wrap_eq := cechCofaceMap_summand_family_comp_eqToHom_eq_summand_family'
155	  (k := k) (C := C) (U := U) s₀ n hn i
156	-- rewrite goal LHS to use wrapper, then exact + scalar pullback chain
157	-- finishes via map_zsmul, smul_comm, h_wrap_pt.
158	sorry  -- becomes: <scalar pullback + map_zsmul + smul_comm + rw [← h_wrap_eq]>
159	       -- + exact h_wrap_pt-derived chain
160	```
161	
162	The precise tactic chain after the lemma is in place will be assembled
163	in-place by the prover; expect ~8–15 LOC after rewriting the goal to
164	match `h_wrap_pt`'s shape.
165	
166	#### Route 3 (FALLBACK) — in-place `convert h_wrap_pt using N`
167	
168	If Route 1's lemma signature does not elaborate cleanly (eqToHom-type-level
169	metavariable issues at the `Pi.hom_ext` step), keep the iter-105 partial
170	proof's setup at L1119–L1144 and replace the trailing `sorry` with:
171	
172	```lean
173	-- Pull (-1)^↑i • out through Pi.π Z₂ j' and eqToHom_outer (k-linear),
174	-- commute past r' • via smul_comm:
175	simp only [map_zsmul]      -- or rw [ModuleCat.hom_zsmul, LinearMap.smul_apply, map_zsmul]
176	rw [smul_comm]
177	-- Then convert into h_wrap_pt:

5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps

15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.

Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures.

21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed

31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors

48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:

51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.

53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."

59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body

72	## LSP MCP Tools: Invocation Rules
73	
74	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls**, not shell commands. Their full names start with `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__` (e.g. `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_local_search`). The lean4 skill reference uses the short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) for readability — those are the **same tools**, never standalone shell binaries.

76	- **Always invoke them through your tool-call interface**, the same way you call `Read` or `Grep`.
77	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` / `lean_diagnostic_messages …` — there is no such shell command and it will fail with `command not found`.
78	- As your **first LSP action**, call `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your assigned file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.

37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.
101	
102	Given that for every affine open `U` of `C.left` there exists a spanning subset
103	`s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` with `Ideal.span s = ⊤` such that the basic-open cover
104	`basicOpenCover s : s → Opens` carries both `IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s)`
105	and `HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)`, conclude `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F`.
106	
107	Iter-055 specialises iter-053's existence-bundled cover form to the basic-open cover
108	shape constructed iter-054. Strategic value: factors out the existence-bundling step
109	from iter-056+ substantive work, so the iter-056+ Koszul acyclicity branch and the
110	iter-057+ Čech-vs-derived comparison branch can each prove specifically the basic-open
111	form (which the universal Koszul + comparison arguments naturally produce), and the
112	iter-055 producer then bundles their results into iter-053's carrier shape automatically.
113	
114	Body: destructures the per-affine existence to obtain `(s, hs, hacy, hcomp)`, then
115	bundles them into iter-053's existential cover via iter-054's
116	`basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top` to produce the supremum hypothesis. -/
117	theorem hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen
118	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
119	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
120	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
121	    (h : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}, IsAffineOpen U →
122	        ∃ (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (_ : Ideal.span s = ⊤),
123	          IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s) ∧
124	          HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)) :
125	    HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F where
126	  exists_cover {U} hU := by
127	    obtain ⟨s, hs, hacy, hcomp⟩ := h hU
128	    exact ⟨s, basicOpenCover s, basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top hU s hs, hacy, hcomp⟩
129	
130	/-- Iter-055: curve specialisation of `hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` at
131	`F := toModuleKSheaf C`.
132	
133	Mirrors the iter-039/042/043/048/049/050/051/052/053 `_curve` pattern: a thin
134	dot-notation wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the
135	structure sheaf) from re-typing `toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-055 producer
136	is chained through. Used by the iter-057+ packaging step to instantiate
137	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` once iter-056+ supplies the
138	per-affine basic-open Čech-acyclic + comparison-iso evidence. -/
139	theorem hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen_curve
140	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
141	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
142	    (h : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}, IsAffineOpen U →
143	        ∃ (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (_ : Ideal.span s = ⊤),
144	          IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s) ∧
145	          HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)) :
146	    HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) :=
147	  hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen h

149	/-- Iter-056: pairwise basic-open intersection identification.
150	
151	Given a Set `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` and indices `i j : s`, the intersection of the
152	two basic-open cover members `basicOpenCover s i` and `basicOpenCover s j`
153	equals the basic open of the product `i.1 * j.1`. Direct iteration of Mathlib's
154	`Scheme.basicOpen_mul`.
155	
156	This is the foundational identification for iter-057+ Čech complex evaluation:
157	the Čech cochain at degree 1 indexes pairs `(i, j) : s × s` and assigns the
158	sheaf evaluated at `basicOpenCover s i ⊓ basicOpenCover s j`. Via this lemma,
159	that intersection is identified with `C.left.basicOpen (i.1 * j.1)`, which by
160	`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (Mathlib) is the localization
161	`Localization.Away (i.1 * j.1)` of `Γ(C.left, U)`. The Koszul-style
162	alternating-sum exactness on these localizations follows from
163	`exact_of_localized_span` (Mathlib) under `Ideal.span s = ⊤`. -/
164	theorem basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul
165	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
166	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
167	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i j : s) :
168	    basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i ⊓ basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s j =
169	      C.left.basicOpen ((i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) * j.1) := by
170	  unfold basicOpenCover
171	  exact (Scheme.basicOpen_mul C.left _ _).symm
172	
173	/-- Iter-056: pairwise basic-open intersection on an affine open is itself
174	affine.
175	
176	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`, `(s : Set Γ(C.left, U))`, and indices `i j : s`,
177	the intersection `basicOpenCover s i ⊓ basicOpenCover s j` is itself affine.
178	Thin wrapper combining `basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul` (rewriting the
179	intersection as a basic open of the product) with Mathlib's
180	`IsAffineOpen.basicOpen` (membership preservation under basic-open formation).
181	
182	Generalises iter-054's `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen` (single-index affine
183	membership) to pairwise intersections. Used by iter-057+ Čech evaluation: each
184	finite intersection of basic opens is itself affine, which is the inductive
185	step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument (Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5
186	/ Eisenbud Commutative Algebra §17 Koszul resolution). -/
187	theorem basicOpenCover_inter_isAffineOpen
188	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
189	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
190	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i j : s) :
191	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i ⊓
192	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s j) := by
193	  unfold basicOpenCover
194	  rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]
195	  exact hU.basicOpen _

199	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
200	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` equals the basic open of the

404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}

412	/-- Iter-087 (refactor): per-summand R-linear restriction-collapse. For nested
413	opens `V ≤ W ≤ U` in a scheme `C.left`, the chain of presheaf restrictions
414	`Γ(U) → Γ(W) → Γ(V)` collapses to the direct restriction `Γ(U) → Γ(V)`.
415	
416	Used in the proof of R-linearity of the Čech differential
417	(`cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`): each per-summand restriction chain
418	`R = Γ(C.left, U) → Γ(C.left, V_{j∘δ_i}) → Γ(C.left, V_j)` collapses to the
419	direct map `R → Γ(C.left, V_j)`, matching the per-i R-action induced by the
420	restriction algebra structure on the cochain factor.
421	
422	Extracted iter-087 from the inline `have R_restrict_R_linear` (iter-086) inside
423	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. The body is the
424	2-tactic proof verified by the iter-086 prover. -/
425	lemma presheafMap_restrict_collapse
426	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
427	    {V W U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
428	    (h_VW : V ≤ W) (h_VU : V ≤ U) (h_WU : W ≤ U)
429	    (r : C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op U)) :
430	    (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VW).op).hom
431	        ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_WU).op).hom r) =
432	      (C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE h_VU).op).hom r := by
433	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
434	  congr 1

1156	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
1157	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
1158	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
1159	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
1160	schemes in our setting.
1161	
1162	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
1163	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
1164	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
1165	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
1166	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
1167	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
1168	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
1169	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
1170	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
1171	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
1172	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
1173	
1174	The structural skeleton is:
1175	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
1176	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
1177	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
1178	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
1179	
1180	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
1181	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
1182	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
1183	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
1184	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
1185	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
1186	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
1187	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
1188	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
1189	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
1190	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
1191	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
1192	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
1193	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
1194	
1195	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
1196	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
1197	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
1198	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
1199	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
1200	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
1201	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
1202	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
1203	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
1204	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
1205	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
1206	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
1207	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
1208	    -- The goal is now
1209	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
1210	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
1211	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
1212	    --
1213	    -- ===========================================================================
1214	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
1215	    -- ===========================================================================
1216	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
1217	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
1218	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
1219	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
1220	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
1221	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
1222	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
1223	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
1224	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
1225	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
1226	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
1227	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
1228	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
1229	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
1230	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
1231	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
1232	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
1233	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
1234	      intro f
1235	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
1236	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.
1237	      -- Plug into `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` with `i₀ := f`,
1238	      -- extract `homotopyEquiv`, op-pass, read off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.
1239	      sorry  -- substep (a) infrastructure: augmented Čech simplicial object
1240	             -- + extraDegeneracyCech + homotopyEquiv + CochainComplex.opEquivalence
1241	    -- ===========================================================================
1242	    -- Substep (b) — Localised-Čech identification.
1243	    -- ===========================================================================
1244	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover Čech cochain complex above is
1245	    -- isomorphic (in `ModuleCat k`) to the localisation at `f` of the
1246	    -- original Čech cochain complex `K`. Cochain-factor-wise:
1247	    --   `(slice cover).X^n = ∏_x F(⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k) ⊓ D(f))`
1248	    --                      = `∏_x F(D(f · ∏ k, (x k).1))`              (Stacks identity)
1249	    --                      = `∏_x Localization.Away (f · ∏ k, (x k).1) Γ(C.left, U)`

Explanation: The rewrite tactic rewrites an expression 'e' using an equality 'a = b' by the following process. First, it looks for all 'a' in 'e'. Second, it tries to abstract these occurrences of 'a' to create a function 'm := fun _a => ...', called the *motive*, with the property that 'm a' is definitionally equal to 'e'. Third, we observe that 'congrArg' implies that 'm a = m b', which can be used with lemmas such as 'Eq.mpr' to change the goal. However, if 'e' depends on specific properties of 'a', then the motive 'm' might not typecheck.

Possible solutions: use rewrite's 'occs' configuration option to limit which occurrences are rewritten, or use 'simp' or 'conv' mode, which have strategies for certain kinds of dependencies (these tactics can handle proofs and 'Decidable' instances whose types depend on the rewritten term, and 'simp' can apply user-defined '@[congr]' theorems as well).

    (hCod : (∏ᶜ ...Fin ((prev n) + 2)...) = (∏ᶜ ...Fin (n+1)...)) :
    (cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ n (Fin.cast hRel.symm i)) ≫ eqToHom hCod
      = cechCofaceMap_summand_family' s₀ n hn i := by
  rcases n with _ | n'
  · omega
  · have hPrev : (ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev (n' + 1) = n' := by
      simp [ComplexShape.prev, ComplexShape.up_Rel]
    sorry
```

Signature elaborates cleanly. After `rcases n with _ | n'` + `omega` (zero case) +
`hPrev : (prev (n'+1)) = n'`, residual goal is the morphism equality between
`cechCofaceMap_summand_family s₀ (n'+1) (Fin.cast _ i) ≫ eqToHom hCod` and
`cechCofaceMap_summand_family' s₀ (n'+1) hn i`.

**Attempts to close the body** (all failed):
1. `refine Limits.Pi.hom_ext _ _ (fun j_new => ?_); rw [Category.assoc]; unfold ...; rw [Limits.Pi.lift_π]` — after unfold + Pi.lift_π on RHS, LHS still has `Pi.lift ≫ eqToHom hCod ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j_new`, can't apply Pi.lift_π directly past eqToHom.
2. `rw [eqToHom_refl, Category.id_comp]` — fails because `eqToHom_refl` requires literal `rfl` syntactic match, not propositional `hCod`.
3. `have hRfl : hCod = rfl := rfl` — fails: LHS/RHS of hCod have different indexing types `Fin ((prev (n'+1)) + 2) → s₀` vs `Fin (n'+1+1) → s₀` until `hPrev` substitutes.
4. `subst hPrev` — fails: would create infinite recursion on `n'`.
5. `simp only [hPrev] at hCod` — `simp made no progress`; `hPrev` doesn't fire on type annotations in hCod.
6. `aesop_cat` — fails to discharge after safe rules.
7. `ext; simp [cechCofaceMap_summand_family, cechCofaceMap_summand_family']` — reduces to per-coord eqToHom-vs-Pi.lift identification; same fundamental gap.

**Root cause**: identifying `eqToHom hCod ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j_new` with
`Pi.π Z_int (j_new ∘ Fin.cast hPrev) ≫ eqToHom (per-coord)` requires
explicit eqToHom-transport-through-Pi.π reasoning that Mathlib does not directly expose
for object-equality eqToHom between products with **different indexing types**.
The standard `eqToHom_naturality` is for naturality of single morphisms,
not for transporting Pi.π through an eqToHom that arises from index-type equality.
